---
title: "Period, GCD, And Prime Full Coils"
---

For positive `n`, the period of stride `k` on `C_n` is `n / gcd(n,k)`. The same gcd counts the number of disjoint stride cycles.

This is where the circle picture and ordinary divisibility meet. If `k` shares a divisor with `n`, the stride steps over the circle in a pattern that only reaches part of it before closing. If `k` is coprime to `n`, the stride has no shared divisor to trap it, so the orbit passes through every node before closing.

Prime circles make this especially clean. On `C_p`, every nonzero stride is coprime to `p`, so every nonzero stride is a full coil. Composite circles have at least one nonzero stride that closes early.

<div class="warning-box">
The widgets expose the gcd and prime/full-coil patterns as executable references. They are not proofs.
</div>

## Picture

<div class="dictionary-box" data-dictionary-id="CC-0205"></div>
<div class="dictionary-box" data-dictionary-id="CC-0206"></div>
<div class="dictionary-box" data-dictionary-id="CC-0207"></div>
<div class="dictionary-box" data-dictionary-id="CC-0208"></div>

<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="CC-T0005"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="CC-T0006"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="CC-T0007"></div>

<div class="widget-panel" data-widget="period_gcd_visualizer" data-theorem-id="CC-T0006" data-dictionary-ids="CC-0205 CC-0207 CC-0208"></div>

<div class="widget-panel" data-widget="prime_full_coil_explorer" data-theorem-id="CC-T0007" data-dictionary-ids="CC-0206"></div>

## Try It

Compare `C_12` and `C_13`. On `C_12`, test strides `2`, `3`, `4`, and `5`; some close early and one is a full coil. On `C_13`, test every nonzero stride and watch the full-coil pattern.

::: {.checkpoint-box}

## Checkpoint

For `C_18` with stride `6`, compute `gcd(18,6)`, the number of disjoint cycles, and the length of each cycle.

:::

## Source Trail

Python reference model:

```python
from circle_math.finite import Circle

C = Circle(13)
all(C.is_full_coil(k) for k in range(1, 13))
```

<p data-paper-ref="papers/PAPER_01_FINITE_CIRCLES.md">Paper source: <a href="https://github.com/corbensorenson/circle-calculus/blob/main/papers/PAPER_01_FINITE_CIRCLES.md">Circle Calculus I</a></p>

<script type="module" src="../../widgets/shared/widget_base.js"></script>
<script type="module" src="../../widgets/S1/period_gcd_visualizer.js"></script>
<script type="module" src="../../widgets/S1/prime_full_coil_explorer.js"></script>
